message:
fix(agent): drain steering messages on provider error to prevent message loss

When a provider call fails, pending steering messages (injected by
extensions or hooks between turns) were being silently dropped because
the error path returned early before draining them. This caused the
agent to miss critical context like updated system prompts or user
corrections that arrived during a failed API call.

Now the error handler drains steering messages and emits proper
MessageStart/MessageEnd events before proceeding with the TurnEnd
error event, ensuring no injected messages are lost regardless of
whether the provider call succeeds or fails.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
